metadata.title: States PCS: Uttar Pradesh GK Questions for UPPSC Examinations

questions.question: Who organized the Mahila Sashastra Dal (Women’s Armed Group) during the 1857 revolt in Kanpur?
questions.explanation: Azizun Bai, a Kanpur courtesan and revolutionary, formed the Mahila Sashastra Dal to support the 1857 uprising.

questions.question: Which of the following districts of Uttar Pradesh is not entirely located within the Eastern Plain agro-climatic zone?
questions.explanation: Only parts of Allahabad district are in the Eastern Plain zone, while Varanasi, Azamgarh, and Faizabad are fully within it.

questions.question: Which state was formed from Uttar Pradesh on 9th November 2000?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand, initially named Uttaranchal, was carved out of Uttar Pradesh on 9th November 2000 as India’s 27th state, primarily comprising its 13 hill districts.

questions.question: In which year was the first Film Policy of Uttar Pradesh introduced?
questions.explanation: The first comprehensive Film Policy in Uttar Pradesh was introduced in 1999. It was later revised in 2013 and 2015 to adapt to evolving trends in the film industry and technology within the state.

questions.question: Arrange these UP districts by highest to lowest population density.

options.label: A
options.text: Ghaziabad, Hapur, Meerut, Gautam Buddh Nagar

options.label: B
options.text: Hapur, Ghaziabad, Gautam Buddh Nagar, Meerut

options.label: C
options.text: Ghaziabad, Meerut, Gautam Buddh Nagar, Hapur

options.label: D
options.text: Hapur, Meerut, Ghaziabad, Gautam Buddh Nagar

answer.label: A
answer.text: Ghaziabad, Hapur, Meerut, Gautam Buddh Nagar

questions.explanation: Ghaziabad has the highest population density (3,967/km²), followed by Hapur (2,000/km²), Meerut (1,346/km²), and Gautam Buddh Nagar (1,161/km²) per Census 2011. All districts are in Uttar Pradesh’s western part, adjacent to the National Capital Region.

questions.question: Who is believed to have founded the city of Kalpi at the end of the 4th century?
questions.explanation: As per local traditions and historical accounts, Kalpi is believed to have been founded by King Vasudeva at the end of the 4th century. This city holds significant historical and mythological importance, and was also referred to as “Kalap Dev ki Kalpi” in ancient sources. King Vasudeva’s rule marked a formative period in Kalpi’s history, linking it with the broader cultural and dynastic developments of early classical India.

questions.question: According to Buddhist texts, who is credited with building the ancient city of Kushinagar?
questions.explanation: According to the Ramayana, Kushinagar (originally called Kushavati) was founded by Kusha, the son of Lord Rama. While the city later became significant in Buddhist history as the place where Lord Buddha attained Mahaparinirvana, its origin is linked to Kusha. Buddhist texts often mention its prominence, and archaeological evidence supports its ancient urban heritage connecting both Hindu and Buddhist traditions.

questions.question: Which English traveler in 1611 recorded Ayodhya’s Ranichand castle ruins?
questions.explanation: William Finch visited Ayodhya in 1611 and wrote about the Ranichand castle ruins. His account notably omits any reference to a mosque, indicating Babri Masjid was not mentioned in sources of that period. Finch was among the earliest English travelers to document Indian historical sites.

questions.question: Which fort did Raja Nain Singh Nagar restore in the 18th century?
questions.explanation: Raja Nain Singh Nagar restored Fort Parikshitgarh in Meerut, Uttar Pradesh, in the 18th century. The British dismantled the fort in 1857 during the Indian Rebellion to prevent its use by revolutionaries. Other forts listed are significant but unrelated to this event.

questions.question: Korma originated in the royal courts of which Indian empire?
questions.explanation: Korma was first prepared in Mughal courts, featuring meat or vegetables braised with yogurt, cream, and aromatic spices. The Mughal Empire significantly influenced Indian cuisine, introducing rich, flavorful dishes like korma to the subcontinent’s culinary culture.
